text:
Once upon a time in a small town named Evergreen, there was an old-fashioned post office. Every Saturday morning you could find Owl Post there sorting letters.

One particular Saturday Owl Post had just opened. Friends brought words—love letters from friends abroad, cards from birthdays. Owl Post read each letter aloud.

There was one lonely letter though.

It read:

Dear Owl Post?

I've never met anybody worth knowing?

Perhaps there must be.

Just somewhere far away.



That sounds lovely.

Why don't we go there?

Perhaps?

Some secrets might surprise us.

Maybe I'll fly you!

So that's just what Owl Post did.

They soared across lakes and forests.

One day...

Oh dear!

There was a wolf!

They flew circles.

No fear.

Maybe I fly you there?

That's wonderful.

Now we find you.

Why?

You know.

That one?

I suppose.

We laugh.




That wolf?

You might guess.

Why?

Because I love birds.

Oh I see.
